title: Splunk APM Database-Span Latency Hotspots, SQL Anti-Pattern Detection, Index-Miss Forensics, and Per-Query Cost Attribution
dataSources: index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces with db.system, db.statement, db.name, db.table, db.operation, db.user, service.name, service.namespace, duration_ms, rows_affected, connection.acquire_time_ms, prepared_statement, db.cassandra.consistency_level, error.type; index=otel_traces sourcetype=otel:span with the same OpenTelemetry database attributes; index=apm_metrics for Splunk APM database MetricSet and db.client style rollups; index=db_logs sourcetype=mysql:slow_query OR sourcetype=postgres:slow_query; lookup db_query_inventory.csv for sql_pattern, expected_p95_ms, owner_app, approved_flag; lookup db_index_inventory.csv for table_name, db_system, index_status, covering_indexes
app: Splunk Observability Cloud (APM), Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry Collector (Splunk_TA_otel)
description: Detects database client span latency hotspots and costly SQL shapes by fusing Splunk APM apm:traces rows, OpenTelemetry otel:span database attributes, Splunk APM MetricSet database metrics in apm_metrics, and mysql or postgres slow-query logs, then scoring tail gaps between P95 and P99, regex-based anti-patterns, bimodal latency_bucket distributions that suggest index or plan instability, connection.acquire_time_ms pool pressure, rows_affected versus duration cost, write-heavy operation mixes, and weak prepared_statement discipline compared to db_query_inventory.csv and db_index_inventory.csv baselines.
value: Product and platform leaders see which exact statement fingerprints and engines consume tail latency budget before user-facing SLOs break, while finance and capacity teams gain per-query cost narratives grounded in volume and duration together. Database owners receive evidence packs that distinguish pool starvation from optimizer regressions, and engineering leaders reduce cross-team debate by joining traces, metrics, slow logs, and curated inventories into one auditable story.
grandmaExplanation: We study the questions your applications ask databases and how long those round-trips take. When a few hidden slow queries, risky SQL shapes, or connection waits threaten customer experience, we surface a clear signal and point teams to the exact statement pattern and database engine involved.
implementation: Land APM db spans in index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces, OTel db spans in index=otel_traces sourcetype=otel:span, database MetricSets in index=apm_metrics, and mysql or postgres slow logs in index=db_logs; maintain db_query_inventory.csv and db_index_inventory.csv. Save apm_uc_13_5_8_db_span_latency_hotspots every fifteen minutes with earliest=-4h@h; page on page or warn severities. Tune span_cnt floors and tail_gap multiples per tier.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability engineers who operate Splunk Observability Cloud APM and Splunk Enterprise trace mirrors, database reliability engineers who own index plans and connection pools, and application teams that ship ORM and raw SQL paths. This use case isolates database client spans described by OpenTelemetry database semantic conventions and Splunk APM exports: db.system names the engine family, db.statement carries normalized or parameterized text, db.name and db.table anchor objects, db.operation separates reads and writes, and duration_ms on the span measures round-trip time from the client driver perspective rather than whole-trace wall time. Splunk APM MetricSets and related database latency rollups in index=apm_metrics provide lower-cardinality confirmation when raw span floods would distort ad hoc percentiles. Slow-query logs in index=db_logs with sourcetype=mysql:slow_query or sourcetype=postgres:slow_query add ground truth when instrumentation truncates statements or when spans are sampled away.

Scope boundaries are explicit so sibling gold use cases stay distinct. UC-13.5.1 remains the authoritative home for service-level trace duration anomalies, dependency-tree slow paths, and tail-sampling dwell without requiring database-span semantics or SQL shape analytics. UC-13.5.2 remains error-rate-first narratives across spans and metrics. UC-13.5.3 remains structural completeness for orphan spans and broken parent links. UC-13.5.4 remains dependency-map topology evidence. UC-13.5.5 remains log-trace correlation mechanics. UC-13.5.6 remains trace fan-out and amplification. UC-13.5.7 remains RED fusion on the service map. UC-13.5.9 and UC-13.5.10 remain real user monitoring angles. UC-13.5.16 remains SLO burn-rate framing. If UC-13.5.3 alarms are chronic, treat SQL anti-pattern detections as hypothesis-only because missing spans can invent false stability.

Prerequisite telemetry quality includes stable db.statement redaction: never ship live national identifiers or primary authentication secrets inside statements; hash literals at the collector when policy requires minimization. Role-based access control should restrict apm and otel_traces indexes to engineering identities with job need. Legal review should approve retention for statement text even when truncated to one hundred eighty characters for display. Agreement on db.system vocabulary matters when comparing postgresql, mysql, mssql, mongodb, redis, cassandra, and snowflake, because optimizer behavior and LIMIT semantics differ materially.

Governance lookups are mandatory before paging. Maintain db_query_inventory.csv with columns sql_pattern, expected_p95_ms, owner_app, and approved_flag so approved analytical shapes do not page during month-end reporting. Maintain db_index_inventory.csv with table_name, db_system, index_status, and covering_indexes so index-miss inference joins to documented expectations rather than guesswork alone. Refresh lookups under change control when migrations land.

Risk briefing for leadership: a rising P99 on a single db.statement while P95 stays calm often means a few gigantic result sets or lock waits dominate the tail, not uniform slowdown. Bimodal latency_bucket counts on the same statement suggest plan instability, partial index use, or replica lag rather than uniform CPU starvation. Elevated connection.acquire_time_ms co-occurring with database duration growth points to pool sizing, network, or broker queueing upstream of the database engine. Cassandra consistency level attributes on db.cassandra.consistency_level interact with tail latency when QUORUM or LOCAL_QUORUM paths stretch under partition pressure.

Differentiation recap: database-span-first analytics with SQL anti-pattern signatures, index-miss forensics via multimodal latency distribution, cross-engine comparison through db.system, pool starvation via acquire time, row-volume cost attribution, read versus write skew, prepared versus ad-hoc mix, fused with MetricSets and slow logs, not generic trace duration paging, not service-map RED, not RUM, not SLO burn primary narratives.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands Splunk APM database spans in index=apm with sourcetype=apm:traces, retaining db.system, db.statement, db.name, db.table, db.operation, db.user, service.name, service.namespace, duration_ms or nanosecond equivalents, rows_affected when exporters provide it, connection.acquire_time_ms when the instrumentation surfaces pool wait separately from server time, prepared_statement boolean or string markers, db.cassandra.consistency_level for Cassandra clusters, and error.type when failures are classified without flooding stack traces into high-cardinality fields. Preserve trace and span identifiers for drill-down even when this rollup search groups them away in stats.

Lane B lands OpenTelemetry database spans in index=otel_traces with sourcetype=otel:span using the same semantic keys, with explicit attention to OTLP attribute flattening: dotted keys may arrive as literal db.system or as flattened variants depending on HEC JSON paths. Normalize at ingest with FIELDALIAS when possible so multisearch arms stay symmetrical.

Lane C lands Splunk APM database MetricSet materializations in index=apm_metrics, including metric_name patterns that include db.client duration families or tenant-specific database rollups your Observability Cloud export exposes. These rows stabilize dashboards when span volume is sampled aggressively. Document which metrics are histogram-derived versus pre-aggregated averages so investigators do not confuse average MetricSet latency with span-level P99 without reconciliation.

Lane D lands slow-query logs in index=db_logs for sourcetype=mysql:slow_query and sourcetype=postgres:slow_query with parsers that extract query text, user, host, and query_time consistently. Align time zones across database hosts and Splunk indexers so slow-log timestamps align with span timestamps when correlating incidents.

HTTP Event Collector tokens for trace JSON require rate limits and dead-letter monitoring. OpenTelemetry Collector processors should batch and attribute-normalize before export. For Snowflake warehouses, ensure db.system is labeled distinctly from generic jdbc aliases so cross-engine comparisons remain honest. For Redis, recognize that command patterns differ from SQL and tune anti-pattern tests that assume SELECT grammar.

Example readiness checks: verify a canary service emits db spans with non-null db.system and duration_ms, verify mysql:slow_query events parse query fields, verify apm_metrics contains at least one database metric series in non-production load tests, and verify db_query_inventory.csv and db_index_inventory.csv load through inputlookup without type errors.

Dashboards should present severity-colored tables by svc, db_system, and stmt fingerprint with drilldowns to raw apm:traces, otel:span rows, apm_metrics series, and db_logs lines for the same window.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as apm_uc_13_5_8_db_span_latency_hotspots on a fifteen minute schedule during steady production, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate svc, stmt, db_system tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, tail_gap, and pool_pressure in pager text so database engineers open EXPLAIN plans and pool dashboards without re-deriving context.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans four arms for apm:traces database spans, otel:span database spans, apm_metrics database rollups, and db_logs slow queries. coalesce ladders absorb nanosecond duration fields, dotted attribute names, and missing rows_affected defaults. bin establishes fifteen minute buckets aligned to APM workflows. stats computes span_cnt, P95, P99, average rows, maximum acquire time, distinct latency_bucket modes, and write_span totals. streamstats builds per svc, stmt, db_system lagged P95 baselines. eventstats adds tbl_med_p95 for table-level context. join with inputlookup db_query_inventory.csv attaches expected_p95_ms and owner_app. join with inputlookup db_index_inventory.csv attaches index_status narratives. case maps tail_gap, anti-pattern flags, pool_pressure, bimodal_hint, write_skew, and ad hoc preparation leaks into page, warn, and info. tstats against the Performance data model adds perf_evt counts as a host-process population probe for investigations that correlate saturation. Final table columns expose operational triage fields including err_types from values of error.type.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.8 APM database spans: p95-p99 tail gaps, SQL anti-patterns, bimodal latency, pool acquire pressure, row-cost. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h stmt_len=180")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces earliest=-4h@h latest=now db.system=*
      | eval db_system=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))))
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,sf_service,""))))
      | eval ns=coalesce('service.namespace',service_namespace,"")
      | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(coalesce('db.statement',db_statement,""))),1,180)
      | eval tbl=coalesce('db.table',db_table,"")
      | eval op=coalesce('db.operation',db_operation,"")
      | eval dbuser=coalesce('db.user',db_user,"")
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round(duration_ns/1000000,4),round(duration_nano/1000000,4),0))
      | eval rows_hit=tonumber(coalesce(rows_affected,db_rows_affected,0))
      | eval conn_acq=tonumber(coalesce('connection.acquire_time_ms',connection_acquire_time_ms,0))
      | eval prep=lower(toString(coalesce(prepared_statement,db_statement_prepared,"unknown")))
      | eval cass_cl=coalesce('db.cassandra.consistency_level',cassandra_consistency,"")
      | eval errt=coalesce('error.type',error_type,"")
      | eval lane="apm_db_span"
      | where len(db_system)>0 AND len(svc)>0 AND isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0
      | fields _time lane svc ns db_system tbl op stmt dbuser duration_ms rows_hit conn_acq prep cass_cl errt ]
    [ search index=otel_traces sourcetype=otel:span earliest=-4h@h latest=now db.system=*
      | eval db_system=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))))
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval ns=coalesce('service.namespace',service_namespace,"")
      | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(coalesce('db.statement',db_statement,""))),1,180)
      | eval tbl=coalesce('db.table',db_table,"")
      | eval op=coalesce('db.operation',db_operation,"")
      | eval dbuser=coalesce('db.user',db_user,"")
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round(duration_ns/1000000,4),0))
      | eval rows_hit=tonumber(coalesce(rows_affected,0))
      | eval conn_acq=tonumber(coalesce('connection.acquire_time_ms',connection_acquire_time_ms,0))
      | eval prep=lower(toString(coalesce(prepared_statement,"unknown")))
      | eval cass_cl=coalesce('db.cassandra.consistency_level',cassandra_consistency,"")
      | eval errt=coalesce('error.type',error_type,"")
      | eval lane="otel_db_span"
      | where len(db_system)>0 AND len(svc)>0 AND isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0
      | fields _time lane svc ns db_system tbl op stmt dbuser duration_ms rows_hit conn_acq prep cass_cl errt ]
    [ search index=apm_metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | where match(lower(metric_name),"db\\.") OR match(lower(metric_name),"database") OR like(lower(metric_name),"%db.client%")
      | eval db_system=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(db_system,"dbms_aggregate"))))
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,sf_service,"apm_metric_host"))))
      | eval ns=coalesce('service.namespace',service_namespace,"")
      | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(dimension_db_statement,sql_fingerprint,"metricset_rollup"))),1,180)
      | eval tbl=coalesce(db_table,dimension_table,"")
      | eval op=coalesce(db_operation,"")
      | eval dbuser=""
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(avg_duration_ms,_value,value,0))
      | eval rows_hit=0
      | eval conn_acq=0
      | eval prep="metricset"
      | eval cass_cl=""
      | eval errt=""
      | eval lane="apm_db_metricset"
      | where duration_ms>=0
      | fields _time lane svc ns db_system tbl op stmt dbuser duration_ms rows_hit conn_acq prep cass_cl errt ]
    [ search index=db_logs (sourcetype=mysql:slow_query OR sourcetype=postgres:slow_query) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval db_system=case(sourcetype=="mysql:slow_query","mysql", sourcetype=="postgres:slow_query","postgresql", true(),"sql")
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,source,"db_log"))))
      | eval ns="slow_log"
      | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(query,slow_query_message,Message,_raw))),1,180)
      | eval tbl=""
      | eval op=""
      | eval dbuser=coalesce(db_user,user,"")
      | eval qsecs=tonumber(coalesce(query_time,Time,0))
      | eval duration_ms=if(qsecs>200,qsecs,qsecs*1000)
      | eval rows_hit=0
      | eval conn_acq=0
      | eval prep="slow_log_adhoc"
      | eval cass_cl=""
      | eval errt=""
      | eval lane="db_slow_log"
      | where len(stmt)>8
      | fields _time lane svc ns db_system tbl op stmt dbuser duration_ms rows_hit conn_acq prep cass_cl errt ]
| eval latency_bucket=case(duration_ms<50,"b_lt50",duration_ms<200,"b_50_200",duration_ms<1000,"b_200_1s",true(),"b_gt1s")
| eval write_span=if(match(lower(op),"insert|update|delete|bulk"),1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count AS span_cnt p95(duration_ms) AS p95_ms p99(duration_ms) AS p99_ms avg(duration_ms) AS avg_ms avg(rows_hit) AS avg_rows max(conn_acq) AS max_conn_acq dc(latency_bucket) AS bucket_modes sum(write_span) AS write_spans values(errt) AS err_types BY _time svc ns db_system stmt tbl op lane
| sort 0 svc stmt db_system - _time
| streamstats window=8 current=f global=f last(p95_ms) AS p95_baseline BY svc stmt db_system
| eventstats median(p95_ms) AS tbl_med_p95 BY tbl db_system
| join type=left max=0 stmt
    [| inputlookup db_query_inventory.csv
     | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(sql_pattern)),1,180)
     | eval expected_p95_ms=tonumber(expected_p95_ms)
     | eval approved_flag=lower(trim(toString(approved_flag)))
     | fields stmt expected_p95_ms owner_app approved_flag ]
| join type=left max=0 tbl db_system
    [| inputlookup db_index_inventory.csv
     | eval tbl=lower(trim(toString(table_name)))
     | eval db_system=lower(trim(toString(db_system)))
     | fields tbl db_system index_status covering_indexes ]
| eval p95_baseline=coalesce(p95_baseline,p95_ms)
| eval tail_gap=if(p95_baseline>0,round((p99_ms-p95_baseline)/max(p95_baseline,0.001),3),0)
| eval anti_select_star=if(match(lower(stmt),"^\\s*select\\s+\\*") OR match(lower(stmt),"select\\s+\\*\\s+from"),1,0)
| eval anti_no_limit=if(match(lower(stmt),"\\bselect\\b") AND !match(lower(stmt),"\\blimit\\b") AND !match(db_system,"^(mongodb|redis|cassandra)$"),1,0)
| eval anti_where_tautology=if(match(lower(stmt),"where\\s+1\\s*=\\s*1"),1,0)
| eval anti_offset=if(match(lower(stmt),"\\boffset\\s+\\d+"),1,0)
| eval anti_cartesian=if(match(lower(stmt),"\\bjoin\\b") AND !match(lower(stmt),"\\bon\\b") AND !match(lower(stmt),"using\\s*\\("),1,0)
| eval bimodal_hint=if(bucket_modes>=3 OR (p95_ms<120 AND p99_ms>1200),1,0)
| eval pool_pressure=if(max_conn_acq>=50 OR max_conn_acq>=0.05*p99_ms,1,0)
| eval row_cost_ratio=if(avg_rows>0,round(avg_ms/max(avg_rows,1),6),0)
| eval write_skew=if(write_spans>0 AND span_cnt>0,round(100*write_spans/span_cnt,2),0)
| eval ad_hoc_share=if(match(prep,"false|0|unknown"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    span_cnt>=60 AND tail_gap>=4 AND p99_ms>2000, "page",
    span_cnt>=40 AND anti_select_star==1 AND p99_ms>=600, "page",
    pool_pressure==1 AND p95_ms>=500, "page",
    span_cnt>=40 AND bimodal_hint==1 AND tbl_med_p95>0 AND p99_ms>(3*tbl_med_p95), "warn",
    anti_cartesian==1 OR anti_where_tautology==1 OR (anti_offset==1 AND p99_ms>=800), "warn",
    anti_no_limit==1 AND avg_rows>10000 AND p95_ms>=400, "warn",
    write_skew>=35 AND p99_ms>=700, "warn",
    ad_hoc_share==1 AND span_cnt>=120 AND match(lane,"apm_db_span|otel_db_span"), "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="page","open_db_explain_plan_index_advisor_and_pool_tuning_runbook",
    severity=="warn","review_statement_text_cache_hit_ratio_and_replica_routing",
    true(),"observe_db_span_tail_and_metricset_drift")
| eval expected_p95_ms=coalesce(expected_p95_ms,250)
| eval approved_flag=coalesce(approved_flag,"false")
| eval slo_breach=if(p95_ms>expected_p95_ms AND approved_flag=="true",1,0)
| eval join_key=1
| join type=left max=0 join_key
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS perf_evt FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-1h@h latest=now
     | eval join_key=1
     | fields join_key perf_evt ]
| table _time svc ns db_system tbl op lane stmt span_cnt avg_ms p95_ms p99_ms tail_gap bucket_modes pool_pressure bimodal_hint anti_select_star write_skew slo_breach severity recommended_action expected_p95_ms perf_evt covering_indexes index_status owner_app err_types
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[apm_uc_13_5_8_db_span_latency_hotspots_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch apm_uc_13_5_8_db_span_latency_hotspots | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time rising, materialize fifteen minute rollups per svc and stmt into a summary index, widen operational alerts to earliest=-2h@h on summaries, and retain this search for deep dives. For very high cardinality statements, replace raw stmt with a server-side hash field at ingest before stats.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in the database engine: capture EXPLAIN or EXPLAIN ANALYZE for a stmt Splunk flags, and confirm whether planner choices match index expectations in db_index_inventory.csv. Compare P95 and P99 from Splunk to metrics the database vendor exposes for the same window when you have access.

Validate field normalization by sampling ten raw events from index=apm and index=otel_traces, confirming duration_ms eval math matches manual nanosecond division within rounding tolerance. Validate slow-log alignment by picking one high-duration span and searching db_logs for overlapping query text fragments.

Validate lookup joins by running inputlookup db_query_inventory.csv alone and confirming sql_pattern keys match truncated stmt forms your instrumentation produces. Validate inventory misses show blank expected_p95_ms before coalesce defaults apply.

Synthetic positive test: in a lab schema, introduce a selective index drop on a narrow table, drive mixed traffic that produces fast index seeks and slow sequential scans on the same statement shape, confirm bimodal_hint or tail_gap escalates after sufficient span_cnt.

Synthetic negative test: on a calm window with approved_flag true and measured P95 below expected_p95_ms, confirm severity stays info for that stmt.

Runbook linkage: tie results to DBA runbooks for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, MongoDB, Redis, Cassandra, and Snowflake that your estate actually runs, including pool sizing guides and read replica failover checks.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — checkout-db span shows tail_gap above four with calm P95: open EXPLAIN, verify accidental sequential scan, restore or add covering index, and compare before-and-after P99 in the same fifteen minute bucket cadence.

Case 2 — ORM emits select star on a wide ledger table: partner with application owners to project columns, add pagination, and re-measure rows_hit versus duration_ms slope.

Case 3 — anti_offset fires on huge table deep pages: replace OFFSET with keyset pagination on primary key or indexed cursor, then confirm anti_offset clears.

Case 4 — pool_pressure with high connection.acquire_time_ms: increase pool limits cautiously, verify connection leaks in application threads, and inspect database max connections.

Case 5 — Cassandra db.cassandra.consistency_level QUORUM spikes during partition repair: coordinate with operators, validate hinted handoff backlog, and tune severity during known repair windows.

Case 6 — Snowflake warehouse suspend and resume causes bimodal db span latency: annotate warehouse auto-suspend policy, exclude cold-start windows with inventory metadata, or route OLTP elsewhere.

Case 7 — Redis command storms without SQL LIMIT semantics: disable SQL-only anti_no_limit logic via db_system match exceptions already in SPL and focus on command cardinality caps.

Case 8 — MongoDB aggregation without indexes shows tail growth: add compound indexes aligned to $match prefixes, confirm winning plan in Mongo explain output.

Case 9 — repeated identical selects in the same trace pattern suggests cache bypass: inspect ORM session settings, second-level cache, and HTTP handler scoping.

Case 10 — slow_log lane disagrees with spans due to truncation: widen slow log verbosity in lab only, align statement hashing at collector, and rely on db_logs for forensic text.

Case 11 — MetricSet lane flat while spans scream tail issues: validate sampling, verify metric_name mapping, and reconcile histogram buckets versus span-level percentiles before muting.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists four arms including index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces db.system=*, index=otel_traces sourcetype=otel:span db.system=*, index=apm_metrics, and index=db_logs slow-query sourcetypes; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; final table includes at least ten analyst-visible columns including err_types and index metadata.

`comment("UC-13.5.8 APM database spans: p95-p99 tail gaps, SQL anti-patterns, bimodal latency, pool acquire pressure, row-cost. Tunables: earliest=-4h@h stmt_len=180")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=apm sourcetype=apm:traces earliest=-4h@h latest=now db.system=*
      | eval db_system=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))))
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,sf_service,""))))
      | eval ns=coalesce('service.namespace',service_namespace,"")
      | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(coalesce('db.statement',db_statement,""))),1,180)
      | eval tbl=coalesce('db.table',db_table,"")
      | eval op=coalesce('db.operation',db_operation,"")
      | eval dbuser=coalesce('db.user',db_user,"")
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round(duration_ns/1000000,4),round(duration_nano/1000000,4),0))
      | eval rows_hit=tonumber(coalesce(rows_affected,db_rows_affected,0))
      | eval conn_acq=tonumber(coalesce('connection.acquire_time_ms',connection_acquire_time_ms,0))
      | eval prep=lower(toString(coalesce(prepared_statement,db_statement_prepared,"unknown")))
      | eval cass_cl=coalesce('db.cassandra.consistency_level',cassandra_consistency,"")
      | eval errt=coalesce('error.type',error_type,"")
      | eval lane="apm_db_span"
      | where len(db_system)>0 AND len(svc)>0 AND isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0
      | fields _time lane svc ns db_system tbl op stmt dbuser duration_ms rows_hit conn_acq prep cass_cl errt ]
    [ search index=otel_traces sourcetype=otel:span earliest=-4h@h latest=now db.system=*
      | eval db_system=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('db.system',db_system,""))))
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,""))))
      | eval ns=coalesce('service.namespace',service_namespace,"")
      | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(coalesce('db.statement',db_statement,""))),1,180)
      | eval tbl=coalesce('db.table',db_table,"")
      | eval op=coalesce('db.operation',db_operation,"")
      | eval dbuser=coalesce('db.user',db_user,"")
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(duration_ms,round(duration_ns/1000000,4),0))
      | eval rows_hit=tonumber(coalesce(rows_affected,0))
      | eval conn_acq=tonumber(coalesce('connection.acquire_time_ms',connection_acquire_time_ms,0))
      | eval prep=lower(toString(coalesce(prepared_statement,"unknown")))
      | eval cass_cl=coalesce('db.cassandra.consistency_level',cassandra_consistency,"")
      | eval errt=coalesce('error.type',error_type,"")
      | eval lane="otel_db_span"
      | where len(db_system)>0 AND len(svc)>0 AND isnotnull(duration_ms) AND duration_ms>0
      | fields _time lane svc ns db_system tbl op stmt dbuser duration_ms rows_hit conn_acq prep cass_cl errt ]
    [ search index=apm_metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | where match(lower(metric_name),"db\\.") OR match(lower(metric_name),"database") OR like(lower(metric_name),"%db.client%")
      | eval db_system=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(db_system,"dbms_aggregate"))))
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('service.name',service_name,sf_service,"apm_metric_host"))))
      | eval ns=coalesce('service.namespace',service_namespace,"")
      | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(dimension_db_statement,sql_fingerprint,"metricset_rollup"))),1,180)
      | eval tbl=coalesce(db_table,dimension_table,"")
      | eval op=coalesce(db_operation,"")
      | eval dbuser=""
      | eval duration_ms=tonumber(coalesce(avg_duration_ms,_value,value,0))
      | eval rows_hit=0
      | eval conn_acq=0
      | eval prep="metricset"
      | eval cass_cl=""
      | eval errt=""
      | eval lane="apm_db_metricset"
      | where duration_ms>=0
      | fields _time lane svc ns db_system tbl op stmt dbuser duration_ms rows_hit conn_acq prep cass_cl errt ]
    [ search index=db_logs (sourcetype=mysql:slow_query OR sourcetype=postgres:slow_query) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval db_system=case(sourcetype=="mysql:slow_query","mysql", sourcetype=="postgres:slow_query","postgresql", true(),"sql")
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,source,"db_log"))))
      | eval ns="slow_log"
      | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(query,slow_query_message,Message,_raw))),1,180)
      | eval tbl=""
      | eval op=""
      | eval dbuser=coalesce(db_user,user,"")
      | eval qsecs=tonumber(coalesce(query_time,Time,0))
      | eval duration_ms=if(qsecs>200,qsecs,qsecs*1000)
      | eval rows_hit=0
      | eval conn_acq=0
      | eval prep="slow_log_adhoc"
      | eval cass_cl=""
      | eval errt=""
      | eval lane="db_slow_log"
      | where len(stmt)>8
      | fields _time lane svc ns db_system tbl op stmt dbuser duration_ms rows_hit conn_acq prep cass_cl errt ]
| eval latency_bucket=case(duration_ms<50,"b_lt50",duration_ms<200,"b_50_200",duration_ms<1000,"b_200_1s",true(),"b_gt1s")
| eval write_span=if(match(lower(op),"insert|update|delete|bulk"),1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count AS span_cnt p95(duration_ms) AS p95_ms p99(duration_ms) AS p99_ms avg(duration_ms) AS avg_ms avg(rows_hit) AS avg_rows max(conn_acq) AS max_conn_acq dc(latency_bucket) AS bucket_modes sum(write_span) AS write_spans values(errt) AS err_types BY _time svc ns db_system stmt tbl op lane
| sort 0 svc stmt db_system - _time
| streamstats window=8 current=f global=f last(p95_ms) AS p95_baseline BY svc stmt db_system
| eventstats median(p95_ms) AS tbl_med_p95 BY tbl db_system
| join type=left max=0 stmt
    [| inputlookup db_query_inventory.csv
     | eval stmt=substr(trim(toString(sql_pattern)),1,180)
     | eval expected_p95_ms=tonumber(expected_p95_ms)
     | eval approved_flag=lower(trim(toString(approved_flag)))
     | fields stmt expected_p95_ms owner_app approved_flag ]
| join type=left max=0 tbl db_system
    [| inputlookup db_index_inventory.csv
     | eval tbl=lower(trim(toString(table_name)))
     | eval db_system=lower(trim(toString(db_system)))
     | fields tbl db_system index_status covering_indexes ]
| eval p95_baseline=coalesce(p95_baseline,p95_ms)
| eval tail_gap=if(p95_baseline>0,round((p99_ms-p95_baseline)/max(p95_baseline,0.001),3),0)
| eval anti_select_star=if(match(lower(stmt),"^\\s*select\\s+\\*") OR match(lower(stmt),"select\\s+\\*\\s+from"),1,0)
| eval anti_no_limit=if(match(lower(stmt),"\\bselect\\b") AND !match(lower(stmt),"\\blimit\\b") AND !match(db_system,"^(mongodb|redis|cassandra)$"),1,0)
| eval anti_where_tautology=if(match(lower(stmt),"where\\s+1\\s*=\\s*1"),1,0)
| eval anti_offset=if(match(lower(stmt),"\\boffset\\s+\\d+"),1,0)
| eval anti_cartesian=if(match(lower(stmt),"\\bjoin\\b") AND !match(lower(stmt),"\\bon\\b") AND !match(lower(stmt),"using\\s*\\("),1,0)
| eval bimodal_hint=if(bucket_modes>=3 OR (p95_ms<120 AND p99_ms>1200),1,0)
| eval pool_pressure=if(max_conn_acq>=50 OR max_conn_acq>=0.05*p99_ms,1,0)
| eval row_cost_ratio=if(avg_rows>0,round(avg_ms/max(avg_rows,1),6),0)
| eval write_skew=if(write_spans>0 AND span_cnt>0,round(100*write_spans/span_cnt,2),0)
| eval ad_hoc_share=if(match(prep,"false|0|unknown"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    span_cnt>=60 AND tail_gap>=4 AND p99_ms>2000, "page",
    span_cnt>=40 AND anti_select_star==1 AND p99_ms>=600, "page",
    pool_pressure==1 AND p95_ms>=500, "page",
    span_cnt>=40 AND bimodal_hint==1 AND tbl_med_p95>0 AND p99_ms>(3*tbl_med_p95), "warn",
    anti_cartesian==1 OR anti_where_tautology==1 OR (anti_offset==1 AND p99_ms>=800), "warn",
    anti_no_limit==1 AND avg_rows>10000 AND p95_ms>=400, "warn",
    write_skew>=35 AND p99_ms>=700, "warn",
    ad_hoc_share==1 AND span_cnt>=120 AND match(lane,"apm_db_span|otel_db_span"), "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="page","open_db_explain_plan_index_advisor_and_pool_tuning_runbook",
    severity=="warn","review_statement_text_cache_hit_ratio_and_replica_routing",
    true(),"observe_db_span_tail_and_metricset_drift")
| eval expected_p95_ms=coalesce(expected_p95_ms,250)
| eval approved_flag=coalesce(approved_flag,"false")
| eval slo_breach=if(p95_ms>expected_p95_ms AND approved_flag=="true",1,0)
| eval join_key=1
| join type=left max=0 join_key
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS perf_evt FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-1h@h latest=now
     | eval join_key=1
     | fields join_key perf_evt ]
| table _time svc ns db_system tbl op lane stmt span_cnt avg_ms p95_ms p99_ms tail_gap bucket_modes pool_pressure bimodal_hint anti_select_star write_skew slo_breach severity recommended_action expected_p95_ms perf_evt covering_indexes index_status owner_app err_types
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS db_host
| table db_host cpu_load mem_used
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries for hosts that run database workloads correlated with APM emitters when you fuse node saturation checks with database span investigations; retain at least fourteen days hot.
visualization: Severity-colored table by svc, db_system, and stmt with drilldowns to raw apm:traces, otel:span, apm_metrics series, and db_logs lines; histogram of latency_bucket modes; dual-axis plot of avg_ms versus avg_rows for row-cost attribution.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Instrument back-end services to export spans to Splunk APM
Splunk Docs — Spans and span tags in Splunk APM
Splunk Docs — Monitor MetricSets in Splunk Observability Cloud
Splunk Lantern — APM monitoring best practices
Microsoft Learn — Query tuning guidance for SQL Server

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate long-running analytical queries against read replicas or Snowflake warehouses often exceed P99 gates while still being approved in db_query_inventory.csv with approved_flag true and elevated expected_p95_ms; tune lookup rows instead of muting the control. Intentional database warm-up after cold cache events, including restart of shared buffers or buffer pool, produces transient tail latency that clears without code changes; correlate with maintenance tickets. Schema migrations that execute DDL and backfills create expected spikes; suppress using change_calendar metadata or environment tags on non-production. Overnight batch windows may dominate rows_hit and duration_ms slopes without implying interactive regressions; split inventories by workload class. Development sandboxes with tiny datasets can make anti-pattern regexes noisy when engineers paste ad hoc SQL; route dev traffic to separate indexes or downgrade severity using environment. Multi-region replica lag can bimodalize read latency on the same statement shape when traffic flips regions; validate replica health before index rebuilds. ORM-generated SQL that appears as select star may still project internally; confirm with application owners before paging. Redis and MongoDB command shapes differ from SQL; rely on db_system exceptions already encoded and extend inventory for engine-specific approved patterns.
hardware: Applies to customer-managed Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud indexes receiving Splunk Observability Cloud APM exports, OpenTelemetry database spans, and database slow-query logs across major relational and NoSQL engines.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.1 trace-duration-first anomaly detection, UC-13.5.2 error-rate-led burn narratives, UC-13.5.3 trace completeness, UC-13.5.4 dependency map analytics, UC-13.5.5 log-trace correlation, UC-13.5.6 trace fan-out, UC-13.5.7 service-map RED health, UC-13.5.9 or UC-13.5.10 RUM, or UC-13.5.16 SLO burn primary framing. Does not mutate database objects; it produces evidence for human-approved tuning.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, ingest apm:traces database spans where one stmt shows P99 above two thousand milliseconds with P95 near two hundred milliseconds for at least sixty spans in fifteen minutes, and confirm apm_uc_13_5_8_db_span_latency_hotspots returns page severity with non-null recommended_action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After tuning when the same stmt shows P99 below five hundred milliseconds with pool metrics calm and approved_flag true in db_query_inventory.csv, confirm severity returns to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows for page and warn filters.

evidence: Saved search apm_uc_13_5_8_db_span_latency_hotspots with scheduled runs; dashboard drilldowns on apm, otel_traces, apm_metrics, and db_logs; archived CSV exports of flagged stmt rows for change review.
